title: vLLM fused_moe CUDA Extension Import Guard for Ascend NPU
problem_description: When vLLM starts on Ascend NPU, the module vllm.model_executor.layers.fused_moe.__init__.py unconditionally imports fused_marlin_moe and fused_moe when HAS_TRITON is True. This triggers a multi-hop import chain: fused_marlin_moe.py → fused_moe.py → _custom_ops → fused_moe_module C++ extension. The compiled fused_moe_module requires the CUDA-specific fused_topk symbol, which does not exist on Ascend NPU. This import failure occurs during platform.pre_register_and_update() at server startup and completely blocks vLLM initialization, preventing model serving. Without this fix, vLLM cannot start on Ascend NPU regardless of the target project.
rough_fix_approach: In vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_moe/__init__.py, identify the block guarded by 'if HAS_TRITON:' that imports fused_marlin_moe and fused_moe, and wrap those import statements inside try/except ImportError blocks so they silently skip on Ascend NPU while remaining available on CUDA. This must coordinate with vllm_ascend's patch_module.py which pre-populates sys.modules with a dummy fused_moe_module to prevent raw C++ symbol lookup errors at a lower level. Verify by checking that vLLM server starts, health probe passes, API validation succeeds, and no CPU/CUDA fallback is detected.

involved_code_files.role: vLLM framework — fused_moe module init that unconditionally imports CUDA-specific C++ extensions (fused_marlin_moe, fused_moe) when HAS_TRITON is True, triggering fused_moe_module C++ extension load needing CUDA fused_topk symbol
vLLM Ascend plugin — pre-existing mechanism that pre-populates sys.modules with a dummy fused_moe_module to prevent low-level C++ symbol lookup errors; collaborates with the import guard fix

reasoning: This is a generic vLLM+Ascend NPU platform incompatibility, not specific to MinerU. Any project that uses vLLM on Ascend NPU and triggers the fused_moe import chain will encounter this failure. The fix is NPU-native (no CPU fallback), verified end-to-end (FULL_PASS gate, health probe, API validation), actionable as a step-by-step procedure, and non-trivial (requires understanding the multi-hop import chain and coordinating with vllm_ascend's patch_module.py dummy-module mechanism). Confidence is 0.85 rather than 1.0 because the exact lines affected may vary across vLLM versions and the conjunction with patch_module.py is version-dependent.
